text: The GB300 is the best AI computer https://t.co/WX6iFyjdJM
summary: The GB300 is the best AI computer
ai_summary: Elon Musk claims the GB300 is the best AI computer, referencing xAI's latest GPU cluster hardware designed for training large language models. This statement relates to xAI's competitive positioning in AI compute infrastructure, highlighting the company's focus on building superior hardware systems for AI workloads—a critical business lever for achieving model training efficiency and maintaining technological advantage in the rapidly competitive AI sector.
embed_html:
The GB300 is the best AI computer https://t.co/WX6iFyjdJM

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 6, 2026

ai_summary: Anthropic is scaling Claude AI inference on SpaceX's Colossus supercomputer to handle growing AI computational demand, leveraging SpaceX's logistics capabilities to manage physical infrastructure scaling. Elon Musk's endorsement highlights the importance of AI safety and team competence in scaling frontier AI systems, demonstrating a strategic partnership between two companies addressing the infrastructure bottleneck in AI deployment at scale.

ai_summary: SpaceX/xAI announced a compute partnership with Anthropic to provide Colossus 1 supercomputer access for Claude Pro and Claude Max improvements. Elon's follow-up reveals a major corporate restructuring: xAI will be dissolved as a separate entity and merged into SpaceX, creating a unified SpaceXAI division—signaling strategic consolidation of AI capabilities within SpaceX's corporate structure.

ai_summary: Anthropic is scaling Claude AI inference on SpaceX's Colossus compute infrastructure, with Elon endorsing Anthropic's safety practices after meeting their leadership team. Elon articulates SpaceX's compute-leasing business model: similar to how SpaceX launches satellites for competitors on fair terms, they will provide AI compute to responsible companies while reserving the right to reclaim resources if the AI poses risks to humanity—establishing a market-based approach to AI safety as a competitive differentiator.

ai_summary: SpaceX is establishing a massive semiconductor manufacturing facility (TeraFab) in Grimes County, Texas, with an estimated $55 billion initial phase investment and $119 billion total investment, representing a major vertical integration move into chip fabrication. Elon's confirmation that this is one of several locations under consideration for 'the largest and most advanced chip fabrication facility in the world' signals SpaceX's strategic pivot to secure advanced computing capacity for its space and AI ambitions, addressing critical supply chain dependencies.
embed_html:
This is one of several locations under consideration for what will be the largest and most advanced chip fabrication facility in the world

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 6, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk responds to criticism about Grok's viability by providing an update on xAI's AI model development strategy: multiple Grok models are currently training simultaneously on Colossus 2 (xAI's supercomputer), with progress also being made on the Grok Built harness. This demonstrates xAI's commitment to advancing its AI product line despite public skepticism, illustrating a key business lesson about maintaining execution focus amid external criticism while transparently communicating infrastructure investments and development progress.

ai_summary: Anthropic is partnering with SpaceX to scale Claude AI inference on Colossus (xAI's compute cluster), leveraging SpaceX's logistics capabilities to handle growing AI compute demands. Elon commits to providing compute access to AI companies meeting safety standards—similar to SpaceX's fair-pricing satellite launch model—while reserving the right to reclaim resources if deployed unsafely, establishing a business framework that ties infrastructure access to responsible AI development practices.
